steps.title: Find the key signal in the hypothesis
steps.content: The political scientists' hypothesis centers on whether announcement timing (before vs. after elections) influences municipalities’ likelihood to respond or offer incentives. The graph must show a lack of significant difference between the two groups to weaken the hypothesis.

steps.title: Match the meaning to the data
steps.content: Choice B states the proportion of responding municipalities and incentives offered was nearly identical between pre- and post-election announcement groups. This directly contradicts the hypothesis that election timing would drive responses. The other three choices focus on either partial data (nonresponse rates in A/D) or internal rates of incentives (C) without comparing across groups. That matches choice B — B is correct.
